Content: [Music] Schafer: You know you got drunk when the next morning
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：你知道自己喝醉了，就是隔天早上

Content: you're just starting to think of things that you said, no.
Content_zh: 才開始想起自己說過什麼。不是吧。

Content: I have this memory just walking down the street yelling and screaming the entire time.
Content_zh: 我只記得自己一路走在街上，一直又喊又叫。

Content: Schafer: I love Chinatown.
Content_zh: Schafer：我愛唐人街。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Did you see how many pages I got done?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：你有看到我寫完多少頁嗎？

Content: So far I've got actual writing.
Content_zh: 目前我真的有寫出東西。

Content: This writing paid for by Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 這些文字是 Kickstarter 出錢讓我寫的。

Content: So, it's a book. It's coming along. We came up with a code name.
Content_zh: 所以，這是一本書。進展還不錯。我們也想出代號了。

Content: Do you want to ask questions or should I just start talking?
Content_zh: 你們想問問題，還是我直接開始講？

Content: Rice: DF adventure. Budget.
Content_zh: Rice：DF adventure。預算。

Content: Schafer: Budget? We need that. We might not need that.
Content_zh: Schafer：預算？這個需要。這個可能不需要。

Content: I think we can have food.
Content_zh: 我覺得吃的可以留。

Content: Launch event.
Content_zh: 發表活動。

Content: I hate to cut the launch event.
Content_zh: 我不太想砍掉發表活動。

Content: That much for PAX Prime really? Rice: Um hm.
Content_zh: PAX Prime 真的要花那麼多？Rice：嗯哼。

Content: Schafer: I mean that, we might not need that.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，這個我們可能不需要。

Content: Let's just say if we're being really bare bones on some of this stuff.
Content_zh: 如果這些東西我們都用最精簡的方式來算。

Content: Rice: We're cutting so much stuff right now.
Content_zh: Rice：我們現在砍掉好多東西。

Content: Rice: Ah, it's the things that like...
Content_zh: Rice：唉，就是那些像是……

Content: Schafer: Greg thought of... Everything I thought of is in.
Content_zh: Schafer：Greg 想到的……我想到的全都留下。

Content: Everything Greg thought of is out.
Content_zh: Greg 想到的全砍掉。

Content: Rice: Yeah. So I was being really conservative on like trying to figure out stuff outside of just paying the members of the team
Content_zh: Rice：對。所以我估得很保守，想弄清楚除了付團隊成員薪水之外，

Content: that we might need to pay for.
Content_zh: 還有哪些東西可能需要花錢。

Content: And some of that stuff might not be necessary.
Content_zh: 其中有些可能不是必要的。

Content: So it's...
Content_zh: 所以這……

Content: Schafer: Well, it's tight for me.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，對我來說很吃緊。

Content: I mean, it's tight for me in terms of like the number of people we have on the team.
Content_zh: 我是說，以團隊人數來看，真的很吃緊。

Content: Like I wish we had more programmers.
Content_zh: 我希望我們能有更多程式設計師。

Content: So if we did that.
Content_zh: 所以如果我們那樣做。

Content: So if we cut out all those, some of those things, we'd have like another $100,000, which would be um...
Content_zh: 如果我們把那些，有些東西砍掉，大概就會多出十萬美元，那就等於……

Content: some programmer time.
Content_zh: 一些程式設計師的工時。

Content: Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: OK. All right.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。行。

Content: And then we have how long to actually make the production.
Content_zh: 那我們實際製作時間有多長？

Content: 'Til next November.
Content_zh: 到明年十一月。

Content: Next November's like infinity far away.
Content_zh: 明年十一月遠得像無限遠。

Content: Rice: It's pretty far.
Content_zh: Rice：是滿遠的。

Content: Schafer: We could do eight games in that time. It sounds so long.
Content_zh: Schafer：那段時間我們都能做八款遊戲了。聽起來超久。

Content: Cut to November.
Content_zh: 畫面切到十一月。

Content: What? The game isn't started yet?
Content_zh: 什麼？遊戲還沒開始做？

Content: Wait.
Content_zh: 等等。

Content: Done with that. We're in Alpha, oh.
Content_zh: 那些都做完了。我們進 Alpha 了，喔。

Content: November this year? Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 今年十一月？Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: We can't do that, it's crazy. That's insane.
Content_zh: Schafer：那不可能，太瘋了。太誇張了。

Content: We're starting in November next year. Rice: No.
Content_zh: 我們明年十一月才開始。Rice：不是。

Content: Schafer: That sounds good.
Content_zh: Schafer：那聽起來不錯。

Content: Rice: That would be a year and a half.
Content_zh: Rice：那樣就是一年半。

Content: Schafer: November this year, oh, that's much closer.
Content_zh: Schafer：今年十一月，喔，那近多了。

Content: Rice: I mean that gives us uhh...
Content_zh: Rice：我是說，這樣我們有，呃……

Content: five, five whole months of production.
Content_zh: 五個，整整五個月的製作期。

Content: Schafer: Five whole months of production?
Content_zh: Schafer：整整五個月的製作期？

Content: Oh my God.
Content_zh: 我的天啊。

Content: Schafer: Well, I'm in an interesting position now because I'm running the company and I'm running a project.
Content_zh: Schafer：我現在的位置滿有趣的，因為我同時在經營公司，也在帶一個專案。

Content: So on the one hand I'm just, I'm equal to the other project leaders and I can't really abuse my ability to like pick up resource up
Content_zh: 所以一方面，我跟其他專案負責人是平等的，不能濫用權力把資源抓過來，

Content: and put them on a different project.
Content_zh: 丟到另一個專案上。

Content: But I do when they're not looking, so that's fine.
Content_zh: 但他們沒看的時候我會這麼做，所以沒問題。

Content: All for the good of the project.
Content_zh: 一切都是為了專案好。

Content: Team Member: Ostensibly, we going to take somebody off your team to put them on DFA.
Content_zh: 團隊成員：表面上，我們要從你的團隊調一個人去 DFA。

Content: And you're going to be short-handed.
Content_zh: 然後你那邊就會人手不足。

Content: Schafer: I mean when you're project leader, you have to kind of fight for your team.
Content_zh: Schafer：當你是專案負責人，就得替自己的團隊爭取。

Content: I mean, it's a fight for resources.
Content_zh: 這就是資源爭奪戰。

Content: And you care about the company overall but you still know that to make your game good you should get the best people on it.
Content_zh: 你會在乎整間公司，但你也知道，要把自己的遊戲做好，就該找最好的人來做。

Content: And so you kind of, you have to do a little recruiting.
Content_zh: 所以你多少得自己招募一下。

Content: You have to talk to them, you know, outside of meetings and stuff and be like, hey, are you interested?
Content_zh: 你得在會議外之類的時候找他們聊，說：「嘿，你有興趣嗎？」

Content: I think we know you'd be, this would be a good opportunity to you.
Content_zh: 我覺得這對你會是個好機會。

Content: You could, you know, you'd be moving up or you'd be taking on more responsibility or we're doing this really cool kind of art or something like that.
Content_zh: 你可能會往上升、承擔更多責任，或是我們在做很酷的美術風格之類的。

Content: Get people excited about it.
Content_zh: 讓大家對它感到興奮。

Content: And mostly we just said that we're doing this project and most people like Anna and Brandon, you know,
Content_zh: 其實大多只是說我們在做這個專案，然後像 Anna 和 Brandon 這些人，

Content: mentioned to me they wanted to work on it.
Content_zh: 就跟我說他們想參與。

Content: Dillon: I mean I was big gamer when I was a kid.
Content_zh: Dillon：我小時候是個重度玩家。

Content: But like adventure games were always kind of special to me in particular.
Content_zh: 但冒險遊戲對我一直特別有意義。

Content: The fact that I wound up, you know, in a configuration where I could get to work on an adventure game with, you know,
Content_zh: 結果我竟然能在這樣的環境裡，參與一款冒險遊戲，

Content: with my favorite adventure game designer.
Content_zh: 而且是跟我最喜歡的冒險遊戲設計師一起做。

Content: It's just crazy and still kind of mind-boggling.
Content_zh: 這真的很瘋，到現在還是覺得不可思議。

Content: Schafer: And because the schedule is so tight on DFA and it's got such a small budget, that's, that is a bit of worry, too.
Content_zh: Schafer：DFA 的時程很緊，預算又很小，這也是有點令人擔心。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Holy shit!
Content_zh: 畫外音：靠！

Content: Schafer: I'm nervous, though. Offscreen Voice: Why are you nervous?
Content_zh: Schafer：不過我很緊張。畫外音：你為什麼緊張？

Content: Schafer: 'Cause I really want Brandon on the team.
Content_zh: Schafer：因為我真的很想讓 Brandon 加入團隊。

Content: And so do the other teams.
Content_zh: 其他團隊也一樣想要他。

Content: So if he does something really awesome, I'm just going to get sad if we then don't get him on the team.
Content_zh: 所以如果他做出很厲害的東西，結果我們沒搶到他，我會很難過。

Content: Dillon: So basically over the weekend I just wanted to prototype some tools work.
Content_zh: Dillon：基本上我週末想做一些工具原型。

Content: Schafer: Me, too. I just had, it was Easter.
Content_zh: Schafer：我也是。只是剛好碰到復活節。

Content: I didn't get around to it.
Content_zh: 我一直沒空做。

Content: Dillon: This is how I celebrate Easter. Schafer: It's beautiful.
Content_zh: Dillon：這就是我慶祝復活節的方式。Schafer：太美了。

Content: Dillon: So I'll show you my super-awesome adventure game here.
Content_zh: Dillon：我來給你看我的超棒冒險遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Oh my God.
Content_zh: Schafer：我的天啊。

Content: Schafer: I didn't know you could draw. Incredible.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道你會畫畫。太驚人了。

Content: Dillon: Yeah, yeah. And I feel like we're basically done.
Content_zh: Dillon：對啊，對啊。我覺得我們基本上完成了。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, we just got to art this up.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，只要把美術補上就好。

Content: Dillon: And so like I started with the classic data editing thing, which is walk boxes.
Content_zh: Dillon：所以我先從經典的資料編輯內容開始，也就是 walk boxes。

Content: And so like got a little, you know, a walk box here.
Content_zh: 所以這裡有個小小的 walk box。

Content: Schafer: Ah, that's crazy. Dillon: Like look how accurate.
Content_zh: Schafer：啊，太扯了。Dillon：你看多精準。

Content: Schafer: Right. Now click on the outer space there. Dillon: It's fine.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。現在點那邊的外太空。Dillon：沒問題。

Content: Schafer: Let me see this. Let me see this.
Content_zh: Schafer：讓我看看，讓我看看。

Content: Dillon: Oh, you're breaking the illusion.. Schafer: Oh, crash.
Content_zh: Dillon：喔，你把幻覺打破了。Schafer：喔，當掉了。

Content: It was a really good puzzle.
Content_zh: 那本來是個很棒的謎題。

Content: Brandon is a genius and a great programmer and also someone who has like a deep love,
Content_zh: Brandon 是個天才，也是很棒的程式設計師，而且他非常熱愛、

Content: a deeper understanding of the low level systems required but also a real affinity for adventure games and tools and pipeline.
Content_zh: 也很懂所需的底層系統，同時對冒險遊戲、工具和 pipeline 都很有感覺。

Content: Dillon: So like this is honestly the stuff that I'm most excited about, right.
Content_zh: Dillon：老實說，這就是最讓我興奮的部分。

Content: It's like, you know, coming up with a tool chain for everybody to use, where like, you know, whatever crazy idea that Tim has, we can get it into the game
Content_zh: 就是做出一套大家都能用的工具鏈，讓 Tim 不管想到什麼瘋狂點子，我們都能把它放進遊戲裡，

Content: as quickly as possible and actually see whether or not it works.
Content_zh: 盡快實際看看行不行。

Content: And tune it up so that it can be as good as it can be.
Content_zh: 再慢慢調整到最好。

Content: I like being behind the scenes.
Content_zh: 我喜歡待在幕後。

Content: Schafer: That's crazy, OK. Programming is actually happening, on the game All right, you guys.
Content_zh: Schafer：太扯了，好。遊戲真的開始寫程式了。各位。

Content: Awesome. That's cool. We're actually doing actual work.
Content_zh: 太棒了。很酷。我們真的在做實際工作。

Content: Schafer: I Probably should go design it now.
Content_zh: Schafer：我大概該去設計它了。

Content: Dillon: It's really nice to just get something on the screen and like that's always the first step to, like it's very clarifying
Content_zh: Dillon：能先讓東西出現在螢幕上真的很好，這永遠是第一步，而且會讓事情變得很清楚。

Content: to, you know, have stuff present and actually interactive, because then it's easy to figure out what to do next basically.
Content_zh: 有東西在那裡，而且真的能互動，基本上就很容易知道下一步該做什麼。

Content: Rice: Oliver. Franzke: Hello!
Content_zh: Rice：Oliver。Franzke：哈囉！

Content: What have you got, man?
Content_zh: 你做了什麼，老兄？

Content: Franzke: It's pretty cool.
Content_zh: Franzke：這滿酷的。

Content: Rice: This is the game?
Content_zh: Rice：這就是遊戲？

Content: Franzke: It's kind of a game. Rice: Oh nice! Walking in 3-D space.
Content_zh: Franzke：算是遊戲吧。Rice：喔，不錯！在 3D 空間裡走路。

Content: Franzke: Well, it's like scaling it vertically, but that's, that's pretty much how we're going to do it in the final game as well.
Content_zh: Franzke：嗯，它其實是垂直縮放，不過最終遊戲裡大概也會這樣做。

Content: Rice: So all these are flat planes and you're just scaling the size of the character?
Content_zh: Rice：所以這些都是平面，你只是縮放角色大小？

Content: Rice: And then we've got Oliver and he's doing a lot of system work on the engine and getting the engine kind of in shape.
Content_zh: Rice：接著是 Oliver，他在引擎上做很多系統工作，把引擎整理到能用的狀態。

Content: Franzke: Well I was born in Germany of course i'm from Germany.
Content_zh: Franzke：我出生在德國，當然，我是德國人。

Content: In fact, from East Germany.
Content_zh: 其實是東德。

Content: So because of historic reasons I can be here today, otherwise I would probably be in Moscow right now, I guess.
Content_zh: 所以因為歷史因素，我今天才能在這裡，不然我現在大概會在莫斯科吧。

Content: I'd probably follow the people that work in the industry, like from a young age, you know, I always was playing games and was interested in the medium.
Content_zh: 我從小就會關注在這個業界工作的人，因為我一直都在玩遊戲，也對這個媒介很有興趣。

Content: It's certainly kind of strange to be here now, to actually write adventure game from scratch.
Content_zh: 現在能在這裡，真的從零開始寫一款冒險遊戲，感覺滿奇妙的。

Content: It's kind of cool, I guess it's what a lot of people in the gaming industry kind of dream about
Content_zh: 這滿酷的，我想這是很多遊戲業的人都會夢想的事。

Content: and would love to do and it's kind of, it's awesome to be here now at this time
Content_zh: 大家都很想做，而現在這個時間點能在這裡，真的很棒。

Content: and like be able to be part of this project, for sure.
Content_zh: 也能成為這個專案的一分子，真的很棒。

Content: It's very exciting.
Content_zh: 非常令人興奮。

Content: I should probably mention that this is not the final game, so please nobody freak out at this point yet.
Content_zh: 我應該先說，這不是最終遊戲，所以現在請大家先不要嚇壞。

Content: Schafer: You know, I've never worked with Oliver before directly.
Content_zh: Schafer：我以前從來沒有直接跟 Oliver 合作過。

Content: He's worked on Once Upon a Monster.
Content_zh: 他做過《Once Upon a Monster》。

Content: And it's funny, he worked on the special edition of Monkey Island back at Lucas.
Content_zh: 有趣的是，他以前在 LucasArts 做過《Monkey Island》的特別版。

Content: Also he's German.
Content_zh: 而且他是德國人。

Content: Franzke: And if I have time, which would be awesome, like to actually dialogue system so you can initiate like a dialogue with some...
Content_zh: Franzke：如果我有時間，那就太好了，我想實作對話系統，讓你可以跟某個……

Content: some other thing in the world.
Content_zh: 世界裡的其他東西展開對話。

Content: Kipnis: So what we're looking at is an implementation of that, of a dialogue sequencer.
Content_zh: Kipnis：所以我們現在看到的是它的一個實作，也就是對話 sequencer。

Content: We have this database of lines.
Content_zh: 我們有這個台詞資料庫。

Content: So i'm just using that to test out my system.
Content_zh: 所以我就用它來測試我的系統。

Content: So I wrote this little test dataset that the red bots are going to insult each other with.
Content_zh: 我寫了一小組測試資料，讓紅色機器人拿來互相辱罵。

Content: And so, yeah, let me show you what it actually looks like when you play it.
Content_zh: 所以，對，我來給你看實際玩起來是什麼樣子。

Content: My friends had Nintendo and that was, that was the best for me.
Content_zh: 我的朋友有任天堂，對我來說那就是最棒的東西。

Content: Like I remember I played a lot of Super Mario Brothers.
Content_zh: 我記得我玩了很多《Super Mario Bros.》。

Content: And I mean, that kind of blew my mind at the time, because when I was growing up I was kind of the oldest kid.
Content_zh: 那時候真的讓我大開眼界，因為我小時候通常是年紀最大的孩子。

Content: And I would come up with games to play with everyone else to entertain them and stuff.
Content_zh: 我會想出遊戲跟其他人一起玩，逗大家開心之類的。

Content: And I realized when I played Super Mario Brothers that this was kind of the same thing.
Content_zh: 玩《Super Mario Bros.》時，我發現這其實有點像同一件事。

Content: Like someone made up like this like little world for other people to experience and to see.
Content_zh: 有人創造了一個小世界，讓其他人去體驗、去看見。

Content: And you didn't even have to be there; And it was just like magical.
Content_zh: 而且你本人甚至不必在場。那就像魔法一樣。

Content: It was like magical technology where I can do this stuff that I had been doing as a child.
Content_zh: 那像是一種魔法科技，讓我能做小時候一直在做的事。

Content: That was like my dream. I got my dream job somehow, basically.
Content_zh: 那就是我的夢想。基本上，我不知道怎麼就拿到了夢想工作。

Content: Schafer: And she, Anna's been implementing my dialogue longer than anybody in the world.
Content_zh: Schafer：Anna 實作我的對話，比世界上任何人都久。

Content: What the hell is that sound? [siren in background].
Content_zh: 那到底是什麼聲音？[背景警報聲]

Content: That happens every Tuesday at noon.
Content_zh: 每週二中午都會這樣。

Content: It's not Tuesday. It is Tuesday.
Content_zh: 今天不是星期二。今天是星期二。

Content: You know, you hear this robot voice?
Content_zh: 你有聽到那個機器人聲音嗎？

Content: Where's the robot voice?
Content_zh: 機器人聲音在哪？

Content: Oh.
Content_zh: 喔。

Content: [faint distorted voice in background].
Content_zh: [背景中微弱扭曲的聲音]

Content: You can barely even hear that.
Content_zh: 幾乎聽不到。

Content: What if that was saying "get out of the building"?
Content_zh: 如果它是在說「快離開大樓」呢？

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我不知道。

Content: It sounds like, like our government has been shut down.
Content_zh: 聽起來像是我們的政府被關閉了。

Content: You are all loyal servants of the...ok so I was saying,
Content_zh: 你們都是……的忠誠僕人。好，所以我剛剛說，

Content: I mean no one's had to implement more of my dialogue ever than Anna, probably because she's been doing it since Psychonauts.
Content_zh: 沒有人實作過比 Anna 更多我的對話，可能因為她從《Psychonauts》就開始做了。

Content: And hopefully on this game, I mean she's... I haven't written a lot of dialogue yet, but she already knows kind of what I'm going to write structurally.
Content_zh: 希望在這款遊戲上，我是說她……我還沒寫很多對話，但她已經大概知道我在結構上會怎麼寫。

Content: And so she's already writing the systems to implement that.
Content_zh: 所以她已經在寫系統來支援那些東西。

Content: Rice: So then we've also got Lee Petty, who is the art director.
Content_zh: Rice：接著我們還有 Lee Petty，他是美術總監。

Content: Petty: It's kind of hard to tune for unless you have...
Content_zh: Petty：除非你有……不然其實滿難調的。

Content: Rice: This is the old one and this is the new one.
Content_zh: Rice：這是舊的，這是新的。

Content: Petty: That's the old one?
Content_zh: Petty：那是舊的？

Content: Rice: This is the old one, yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：這是舊的，對。

Content: Petty: That's a little different than I remember it.
Content_zh: Petty：這跟我記得的有點不一樣。

Content: Petty: I've pretty much been in the game industry since 1996 constantly.
Content_zh: Petty：我差不多從 1996 年開始就一直待在遊戲業。

Content: I was at Accolade for about five years.
Content_zh: 我在 Accolade 待了大約五年。

Content: I worked on a variety of projects there, some of which shipped, some of which didn't.
Content_zh: 我在那裡做過各種專案，有些有推出，有些沒有。

Content: I actually was cursed.
Content_zh: 我其實被詛咒了。

Content: I don't remember how many years, but I think it was like my first three years in the industry,
Content_zh: 我不記得持續幾年，但我想大概是我進業界的前三年，

Content: everything I worked on was cancelled.
Content_zh: 我做的東西全都被取消。

Content: It was like, ahhh.
Content_zh: 那感覺就是，啊啊啊。

Content: I can't get a single game on my resume.
Content_zh: 我的履歷上一款遊戲都放不上去。

Content: Luckily games didn't take three years to make each back then, right?
Content_zh: 幸好那時候遊戲不會每款都做三年，對吧？

Content: Schafer: Yeah, Lee's been here for a while.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，Lee 在這裡待一陣子了。

Content: He was, came in, in the middle of Brutal Legend and took it over and made it look good.
Content_zh: 他是在《Brütal Legend》做到一半時進來接手，然後把它弄得很好看。

Content: He's been in the industry for a long time.
Content_zh: 他在這個業界很久了。

Content: I think if you look at him you can see the, it's a wear on his face from the games industry.
Content_zh: 我覺得你看他的臉，就看得出遊戲業留下的磨損。

Content: No, he's worked on the games for a long time.
Content_zh: 不是啦，他做遊戲做很久了。

Content: He knows, he knows everything.
Content_zh: 他什麼都懂。

Content: He's, you know, he's run his own projects.
Content_zh: 他也帶過自己的專案。

Content: He ran Stacking all by himself. I didn't get involved in that at all.
Content_zh: 《Stacking》就是他自己帶的。我完全沒有參與。

Content: He wrote the dialogue for it. He did everything.
Content_zh: 他寫了對話，什麼都做了。

Content: So we're lucky to have him on projects for a little while.
Content_zh: 所以能讓他暫時加入這個專案，我們很幸運。

Content: I'm trying to figure out whether we can steal him for good or not.
Content_zh: 我正在想能不能把他永久偷過來。

Content: But there's so many other things we need him to do.
Content_zh: 但我們還有好多其他事情需要他做。

Content: Don't let him see this footage, though, 'cause he'll ask for a raise.
Content_zh: 不過別讓他看到這段影片，因為他會要求加薪。

Content: Rice: Yeah. We're starting to get like a team on the game.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我們開始真的有一支遊戲團隊了。

Content: Schafer: This is the nightmare team from hell, you guys.
Content_zh: Schafer：各位，這是地獄來的惡夢團隊。

Content: You've never seen a sicker, crazier team.
Content_zh: 你們沒見過比這更病、更瘋的團隊。

Content: Petty: I'm getting Wi-Fi but it's not connecting to the Skype.
Content_zh: Petty：我有收到 Wi-Fi，但連不上 Skype。

Content: Did you, you signed out on purpose, right?
Content_zh: 你是故意登出的，對吧？

Content: Rice: Yeah, because it went away.
Content_zh: Rice：對，因為它不見了。

Content: Petty: Yeah, I don't know what's going on.
Content_zh: Petty：對，我不知道怎麼回事。

Content: Skype server seemed like they're having issues.
Content_zh: Skype 伺服器好像有問題。

Content: Schafer: OK, this is the first stand-up. Isn't it exciting?
Content_zh: Schafer：好，這是第一次站會。是不是很興奮？

Content: We can't do it without Bagel. You can't get Bagel?
Content_zh: 沒有 Bagel 我們不能開始。你叫不到 Bagel 嗎？

Content: Rice: Internet's being weird.
Content_zh: Rice：網路怪怪的。

Content: Kipnis: Should we just call him?
Content_zh: Kipnis：要不要直接打給他？

Content: Schafer: Bagel, we're having troubles with Skype.
Content_zh: Schafer：Bagel，我們 Skype 有問題。

Content: Can we just speaker-phone you in?
Content_zh: 可以直接用擴音電話讓你加入嗎？

Content: Bagel: Yeah, let's do it.
Content_zh: Bagel：可以，就這樣吧。

Content: Schafer: OK, we're doing it.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，我們要這樣做了。

Content: Rice: Is that all the way up?
Content_zh: Rice：音量開到最大了嗎？

Content: Schafer: That's as loud as it gets.
Content_zh: Schafer：已經最大聲了。

Content: Say hi, Bagel. Bagel: Hey, everybody.
Content_zh: 跟大家打招呼，Bagel。Bagel：大家好。

Content: Rice: And then we've also got, we've always had Bagel on, doing like concept arts and backgrounds and stuff like that.
Content_zh: Rice：然後我們也一直有 Bagel，負責概念美術、背景之類的東西。

Content: But now we've got an art director on the project that'll actually start trying to take those images that Bagel has made
Content_zh: 但現在專案上有美術總監，會開始把 Bagel 畫的那些圖，

Content: and figure out how to actually get them in a game and get them to be like working game assets.
Content_zh: 想辦法真的放進遊戲裡，變成可運作的遊戲素材。

Content: Schafer: Bagel Stapley, Nathan.
Content_zh: Schafer：Bagel Stapley，Nathan。

Content: Stapley: Buddy.
Content_zh: Stapley：老兄。

Content: Schafer: Look at that baaaaaaa. Lee Petty is here.
Content_zh: Schafer：看看那個 Baaaaaaa。Lee Petty 也在。

Content: Petty: What's up, Bagel?
Content_zh: Petty：最近怎樣，Bagel？

Content: Schafer: Lee Petoir is here.
Content_zh: Schafer：Lee Petoir 也在。

Content: Stapley: Can you hear me? How is it?
Content_zh: Stapley：聽得到我嗎？效果怎樣？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. I can hear you, can you hear me?
Content_zh: Schafer：可以。我聽得到你，你聽得到我嗎？

Content: Stapley: Yeah, I can hear you.
Content_zh: Stapley：可以，我聽得到。

Content: Schafer: Oh, man. I haven't paid my property taxes.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊。我還沒繳房產稅。

Content: So embarrassing.
Content_zh: 真尷尬。

Content: So one of the main things I want to do is pick what you're going to paint for Lee to mess with in after-effects.
Content_zh: 我想做的主要事情之一，是選出你要畫什麼，讓 Lee 在 After Effects 裡拿去處理。

Content: Stapley: Yeah. Schafer: Remember that? Stapley: Yeah.
Content_zh: Stapley：對。Schafer：記得嗎？Stapley：記得。

Content: So is that how we're going to do it?
Content_zh: 所以我們要這樣做嗎？

Content: Like I'm thinking of layers basically.
Content_zh: 我基本上是在想分層。

Content: Petty: The goal is just to get like a type of environment, you know.
Content_zh: Petty：目標只是先做出一種環境。

Content: Like maybe it looks like something that the player could walk, kind of left or right and have it scroll or into.
Content_zh: 看起來像玩家可以往左或往右走，讓畫面捲動，或是往裡走的地方。

Content: Kind of either one we could explore.
Content_zh: 兩種方向我們都可以試。

Content: Schafer: Right now he's taking, you know, Bagel's work and Bagel's not a very technical person
Content_zh: Schafer：他現在是在拿 Bagel 的作品來做，而 Bagel 不是很技術型的人。

Content: so he just paints these awesome paintings.
Content_zh: 所以他就是畫出這些很棒的畫。

Content: But then making it actually look good in the game is a whole different thing.
Content_zh: 但要讓它在遊戲裡真的好看，是完全不同的一回事。

Content: And so we've got Lee working on actually making that a realized game playing world.
Content_zh: 所以我們請 Lee 來把它做成真正可遊玩的遊戲世界。

Content: And it's really fun to see them working together, because they're so different but they're putting together this, I think,
Content_zh: 看他們一起工作真的很好玩，因為他們很不一樣，但他們正在拼出一種我覺得

Content: awesome looking visual style.
Content_zh: 看起來很棒的視覺風格。

Content: Schafer: There's a lot of cabins in here.
Content_zh: Schafer：這裡有很多小屋。

Content: Petty: Hey, that'd be a good one, Tim. That cabin.
Content_zh: Petty：嘿，那個不錯，Tim。那間小屋。

Content: So if you wanted the player walk into the screen there, like it's a little slightly different
Content_zh: 如果你想讓玩家往畫面裡走，那有點不太一樣，

Content: but similar to that whole circus sequence in Monkey one.
Content_zh: 但有點像《Monkey Island》一代裡整段馬戲團那樣。

Content: You just walk into that and they can make a move up to that top shot basically.
Content_zh: 你直接走進去，基本上就能往上移到那個俯視鏡頭。

Content: Petty: We haven't designed in the actual characters yet for the game.
Content_zh: Petty：我們還沒有設計遊戲裡真正的角色。

Content: Tim is still writing and coming up with exactly what the world is.
Content_zh: Tim 還在寫，也還在構思這個世界到底是什麼。

Content: So we just sort of picked a character out of one of Bagel's sketches and put it into the game.
Content_zh: 所以我們只是從 Bagel 的一張草圖裡挑了個角色，放進遊戲裡。

Content: And we needed to do, you know, we just needed it to move around.
Content_zh: 我們需要做的只是讓它能到處移動。

Content: So I just did some really quick animation on it.
Content_zh: 所以我就很快替它做了一點動畫。

Content: It's certainly not great animation but just to get it to kind of move around the world.
Content_zh: 那當然不是什麼很棒的動畫，只是讓它能在世界裡移動。

Content: And follow the camera.
Content_zh: 並且跟著攝影機走。

Content: And so I think, you know, you kind of get a sense of, is how the camera might follow the player and kind of lag behind the player.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得你可以大概感受到攝影機會怎麼跟著玩家，並稍微落後於玩家。

Content: How it might move around the screen.
Content_zh: 它可能會怎麼在畫面上移動。

Content: And you'll see that one of the things we're doing here with this character is that as he's in this canopy, he's kind of tinted green and darker.
Content_zh: 你會看到我們在這個角色上做的一件事，是當他在樹蔭下時，顏色會偏綠、偏暗。

Content: And as he emerges into the sun, here he becomes a little brighter.
Content_zh: 當他走到陽光下，這裡就會變亮一點。

Content: And we don't really have, you know, any true lighting that's going to paint it into the painting,
Content_zh: 我們其實沒有真正的燈光，因為那些光影會畫在圖裡。

Content: but I'm doing some simple things where we're using like color gradients based on location of the world and kind of multiplying it in this character.
Content_zh: 但我做了一些簡單處理，根據角色在世界中的位置使用色彩漸層，再疊加到角色身上。

Content: Just with the goal of getting to feel part of that environment.
Content_zh: 目的只是讓他感覺像是環境的一部分。

Content: And that's a type of trick that we could do technically in the game image if we wanted to.
Content_zh: 如果我們想的話，技術上也可以在遊戲畫面裡做這種技巧。

Content: And it's really quick to do.
Content_zh: 而且做起來很快。

Content: But it's enough to help him harmonize with the environment.
Content_zh: 但已經足夠讓他跟環境融合。

Content: Schafer: Please join me in welcoming Mr. Chris Remo to the company.
Content_zh: Schafer：請大家和我一起歡迎 Chris Remo 先生加入公司。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: Remo's our community manager.
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：Remo 是我們的社群經理。

Content: He's been here now for two weeks or so, ish.
Content_zh: 他來這裡大概兩週左右，差不多。

Content: Chris, could you please stand up.
Content_zh: Chris，可以請你站起來嗎？

Content: [Laughter] Any questions about any of that stuff before we talk about projects?
Content_zh: [笑聲] 在我們談專案之前，對剛剛那些事有問題嗎？

Content: I'll give you the update on Double Fine Adventures, awesome game.
Content_zh: 我來更新一下《Double Fine Adventure!》，這款超棒遊戲。

Content: I'm working on design stuff and we have a team actually working on tools and engine and visual,
Content_zh: 我正在做設計方面的事，我們也有團隊實際在做工具、引擎和視覺，

Content: pre-visualization of what the art side will look like.
Content_zh: 也就是美術方向會長什麼樣子的預視。

Content: And I'm putting together one of my deliverables for the milestone, in a couple weeks.
Content_zh: 我也在整理幾週後里程碑要交的一項成果。

Content: It's like a story document and stuff.
Content_zh: 像是故事文件之類的。

Content: And at that point I'll be like probably sharing more info about the game with other people company-wide.
Content_zh: 到那時我可能就會跟公司裡其他人分享更多遊戲資訊。

Content: Rice: Well I mean, Tim's been doing brainstorming stuff for a while.
Content_zh: Rice：我是說，Tim 已經腦力激盪一陣子了。

Content: And that's still what he's doing, just kind of holing up in his office and figuring out what the game is.
Content_zh: 他現在也還是在做這件事，就是把自己關在辦公室裡，想清楚這款遊戲是什麼。

Content: Schafer: You know, at the very beginning I'm locked in my room a lot writing dialogue.
Content_zh: Schafer：一開始我常常把自己關在房間裡寫對話。

Content: But then I kind of have the job of pulling this whole thing down the road.
Content_zh: 但接下來我的工作就是把整件事往前推。

Content: You know, so I was ready to finally pitch to the team.
Content_zh: 所以我終於準備好向團隊提案了。

Content: I'm not really, haven't really felt right to pitch to the whole company yet.
Content_zh: 我還沒覺得適合對全公司提案。

Content: That'll come later but, so I pitched to the whole team.
Content_zh: 那之後再說，所以我先對整個團隊提案。

Content: Schafer: OK. So I'm going to talk about the game today with everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。所以我今天要跟大家談談這款遊戲。

Content: And some people have heard about it and some people have not.
Content_zh: 有些人聽過，有些人還沒有。

Content: Did I talk to you about the game?
Content_zh: 我有跟你談過這款遊戲嗎？

Content: Some people have not, not just you.
Content_zh: 有些人還沒聽過，不只你。

Content: [Laughter] Petty: There're several people in Wisconsin.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Petty：威斯康辛還有好幾個人。

Content: Schafer: As you see, I've made a lot of progress.
Content_zh: Schafer：如你們所見，我進展很多。

Content: I've designed some monster designs. Rice: I like it.
Content_zh: 我設計了一些怪物設計。Rice：我喜歡。

Content: Schafer: And then I was going to go and do some detail about some ideas for the beginning of one side of the story.
Content_zh: Schafer：接著我想稍微詳細說明其中一邊故事開頭的一些想法。

Content: Just 'cause the first example, like a sample game play and then we can all tear it apart
Content_zh: 就當作第一個例子，像一段範例玩法，然後大家可以一起把它拆爛。

Content: and talk about how it's never, never, never going to work.
Content_zh: 討論它到底為什麼絕對、絕對、絕對行不通。

Content: Schafer: And it's an interesting process where...
Content_zh: Schafer：這是個很有趣的過程，因為……

Content: deciding when to solicit feedback and when to actually sell.
Content_zh: 你得決定什麼時候徵求意見，什麼時候其實是在推銷。

Content: It's like sometimes you're selling and sometimes looking for feedback.
Content_zh: 有時候你是在推銷，有時候你是在找回饋。

Content: And the idea... And early on I could sense like in the meeting, 'cause we talked about that as being a feedback session about the pitch and stuff.
Content_zh: 而那個想法……早期我在會議裡就感覺得到，因為我們說那會是針對提案的回饋會議。

Content: I realized I didn't want any feedback right then, like any in-depth feedback.
Content_zh: 但我發現我當下不想要任何回饋，尤其是深入回饋。

Content: So I kind of pitched the game and then kind of moved the meeting forward and ended it and because there is an early stage
Content_zh: 所以我大概提了一下遊戲，然後就把會議往前帶，結束掉，因為在早期階段，

Content: where you don't want any feedback because it doesn't really mean anything.
Content_zh: 你不會想要任何回饋，因為那些其實沒什麼意義。

Content: Because they don't know still entirely what you're thinking and they might have an image in their head
Content_zh: 因為他們還不完全知道你在想什麼，而他們腦中可能有個畫面，

Content: but it probably isn't exactly like what I have in my head.
Content_zh: 但那很可能跟我腦中的東西不太一樣。

Content: So I almost don't want their opinion of it because they're not talking about the thing I'm talking about.
Content_zh: 所以我幾乎不想聽他們對它的看法，因為他們談的不是我正在談的東西。

Content: They're talking about their own, how they see it.
Content_zh: 他們談的是自己的版本，是他們怎麼看它。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Can I contribute as a devil's advocate?
Content_zh: 畫外音：我可以從反方角度提點意見嗎？

Content: Schafer: I like where this is going.
Content_zh: Schafer：我喜歡這個走向。

Content: Remo: To me the strongest parts of this by far are the thematic aspects of the two characters in parallel.
Content_zh: Remo：對我來說，這裡最強的部分遠遠是兩個角色平行對照的主題性。

Content: Especially switching between those, I think, could be incredibly important and really, really cool.
Content_zh: 特別是在兩者之間切換，我覺得可能非常重要，也真的很酷。

Content: Both in terms of game play and aesthetics and the stuff supporting that seems so high-concept to me that it might end up being top-heavy.
Content_zh: 不論玩法或美學都是如此，但支撐它的那些東西對我來說概念太高，最後可能會頭重腳輕。

Content: Like it seems, it just seems like a really complicated thing.
Content_zh: 它看起來，真的就是很複雜。

Content: Schafer: People at, you know, at that meeting can bring up any sort of problems but it's just too early, you know.
Content_zh: Schafer：會議上的人可以提出各種問題，但現在真的太早了。

Content: I dare them. Nice try, Chris Remo.
Content_zh: 我倒要看看。想得美，Chris Remo。

Content: What were we thinking inviting that guy to the meeting?
Content_zh: 我們到底為什麼會邀那傢伙來開會？

Content: Hey, maybe this thing you're doing shouldn't be done. Thanks.
Content_zh: 嘿，也許你們正在做的東西根本不該做。謝啦。

Content: Remo: That's... I mean...
Content_zh: Remo：那是……我是說……

Content: Schafer: I guess because I see it working in my head, it isn't that complicated.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想是因為它在我腦中運作得起來，所以它沒那麼複雜。

Content: I see that being like just providing a structure, actually, to how everything has to behave.
Content_zh: 我覺得它其實只是替所有東西該怎麼運作提供一個結構。

Content: Schafer: I do want is like when I say the idea, what other ideas that makes that person think of.
Content_zh: Schafer：我真正想要的是，當我說出這個想法，它會讓那個人聯想到什麼其他點子。

Content: Like you know what would be cool? And how they want to add to it.
Content_zh: 像是「你知道什麼會很酷嗎？」然後他們想怎麼加上去。

Content: Petty: I love hearing new ideas.
Content_zh: Petty：我很喜歡聽新點子。

Content: I like, Tim is a very, a lot of times Tim, the way he describes ideas is like, like the fact he described a box cover for a game
Content_zh: 我喜歡，Tim 很……很多時候 Tim 描述想法的方式，比方說他會描述一款遊戲的盒裝封面，

Content: that doesn't exist is a very Tim thing to do.
Content_zh: 而那款遊戲根本不存在，這就很 Tim。

Content: Like he has, he's not an artist per se, like visual artist, but he thinks very visually.
Content_zh: 他本身不是藝術家，不是視覺藝術家，但他思考方式很視覺化。

Content: And I think that's helpful for someone like me and it's probably why we work so well together, is, you know,
Content_zh: 我覺得這對我這種人很有幫助，也可能是我們合作得這麼好的原因。

Content: he's got these ideas and I can sort of, you know, as he's drawing out the birthday cakes and stuff like that
Content_zh: 他有這些點子，當他畫出生日蛋糕之類的東西時，

Content: I probably shouldn't talk about...
Content_zh: 我大概不該講這個……

Content: As he's drawing out some of the ideas on the white board in really simple fashion, I'm immediately starting to think about like,
Content_zh: 當他用很簡單的方式在白板上畫出一些想法時，我馬上就開始想，

Content: well, how's that going to put together in the space and how can we emphasize that or make that work.
Content_zh: 那在空間裡要怎麼組起來？我們要怎麼強調它，或讓它真的可行？

Content: And so that's exciting to hear.
Content_zh: 所以聽到那些會很興奮。

Content: Schafer: Anyway. So you pick which side you're on.
Content_zh: Schafer：總之，你要選自己站在哪一邊。

Content: I've only been, I've been working on this one a little bit.
Content_zh: 我只有，這邊我稍微做了一點。

Content: So imagine it would just kind of scroll the boy off the screen.
Content_zh: 想像一下，它會把男孩捲出畫面。

Content: And she would be sitting here leaning on this tree on a hillside.
Content_zh: 然後她會坐在這裡，靠在山坡上的這棵樹旁。

Content: My drawing gets worse and worse the more people are watching.
Content_zh: 越多人看，我畫得越爛。

Content: Kipnis: Are those her legs? Schafer: It could be Lee: She's a tick.
Content_zh: Kipnis：那是她的腿嗎？Schafer：有可能。Lee：她是隻蜱。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, turns out she's a tick in the end.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，最後發現她其實是隻蜱。

Content: Kipnis: They're both human. Schafer: They're human, yeah, yeah, they're human.
Content_zh: Kipnis：他們都是人類。Schafer：他們是人類，對，對，他們是人類。

Content: Petty: Yeah, I think the idea's really cool and I think visually, I think there's a lot of interesting stuff that we have,
Content_zh: Petty：對，我覺得這個點子很酷，而且從視覺上看，我覺得裡面有很多有趣的東西。

Content: I haven't really had a chance to talk to Tim yet, but I think there's a lot of interesting stuff thematically
Content_zh: 我還沒有機會好好跟 Tim 談，但我覺得主題上有很多有趣的東西，

Content: that is appealing to me.
Content_zh: 很吸引我。

Content: I mean, there's this sort of almost Tale of Two Cities quality to it.
Content_zh: 我是說，它有一種近似《雙城記》的特質。

Content: I can see a lot of good ambiguity in the way that that story is structured.
Content_zh: 我可以從那個故事結構裡看到很多很好的曖昧性。

Content: I think that's really, will be great.
Content_zh: 我覺得那真的會很棒。

Content: And to be a way that we, from a design and narrative way move the genre forward a little bit.
Content_zh: 也可以讓我們從設計和敘事上，稍微把這個類型往前推一點。

Content: Kipnis: I want, I want something new to be brought to the table that wasn't there before.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我希望能帶來一些以前沒有的新東西。

Content: And I'm not sure what that is quite yet because I still don't have like a very clear idea of what the game's going to be like.
Content_zh: 我還不太確定那是什麼，因為我對這款遊戲會是什麼樣子還沒有很清楚的概念。

Content: But hopefully I can contribute to that.
Content_zh: 但希望我能對那件事有所貢獻。

Content: Franzke: Yeah, I know, I'm confident from a technology point of view be able to do what is necessary.
Content_zh: Franzke：對，我知道。從技術角度來看，我有信心能做出需要的東西。

Content: I hope it's going to be good.
Content_zh: 希望它會很好。

Content: [Music] Schafer: All right. Rice: Wait you forgot the big thing.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：好。Rice：等等，你忘了重點。

Content: Rice: Bar crawl.
Content_zh: Rice：酒吧巡禮。

Content: Schafer: Who can go to the bar crawl tonight?
Content_zh: Schafer：今晚誰能去酒吧巡禮？

Content: Petty: I can't go tonight. I'm too sick, actually. I have to go home and sleep.
Content_zh: Petty：我今晚不能去。其實我病得太嚴重了，得回家睡覺。

Content: [Music] Offscreen Voice: Actually, Tim, can you just say what we're doing again?
Content_zh: [音樂] 畫外音：其實 Tim，你可以再說一次我們在做什麼嗎？

Content: Schafer: Where... you don't know where we're going?
Content_zh: Schafer：去哪……你不知道我們要去哪？

Content: Kipnis: It's for the camera.
Content_zh: Kipnis：這是給攝影機拍的。

Content: But you know, should I make sound really natural like I'm just happy to be talking about it?
Content_zh: 不過你知道，我是不是該講得很自然，好像我只是很開心要談這件事？

Content: Kipnis: Hey Tim, where are we going, by the way? Do you know?
Content_zh: Kipnis：嘿 Tim，順便問一下，我們要去哪？你知道嗎？

Content: Schafer: What? Kipnis: Where are we going?
Content_zh: Schafer：什麼？Kipnis：我們要去哪？

Content: Schafer: I don't know. Where are we going? No.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道。我們要去哪？不是。

Content: Tonight is a very special night because we are going to retrace the history of Double Fine
Content_zh: 今晚是很特別的一晚，因為我們要回顧 Double Fine 的歷史，

Content: through the code names of our projects.
Content_zh: 透過我們各個專案的代號來回顧。

Content: Shall we do that again?
Content_zh: 我們要再來一次嗎？

Content: Projects.
Content_zh: 專案。

Content: The history of our company as told by the code names of our projects, which are mostly taken from Chinatown dive bars.
Content_zh: 用我們專案代號說出的公司歷史，而那些代號大多取自唐人街的小酒吧。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Why don't you tell us about what happened last night?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你何不跟我們說說昨晚發生了什麼？

Content: Schafer: You mean between me and, oh, oh.
Content_zh: Schafer：你是說我跟，喔，喔。

Content: Last night.
Content_zh: 昨晚。

Content: So we went on a quest last night to find a code name for the project.
Content_zh: 我們昨晚展開了一場任務，要替這個專案找代號。

Content: It's fun. I mean we used to, like in the very beginning of the company,
Content_zh: 這很好玩。公司最早期的時候，

Content: when we only like, the whole company was like twelve people, we'd go out a lot together.
Content_zh: 整間公司大概只有十二個人，我們常常一起出去。

Content: And go bar crawling together and we hadn't done that a lot lately.
Content_zh: 一起跑酒吧，但最近很少這麼做了。

Content: But it was fun to get back out there again with the team.
Content_zh: 不過能再跟團隊一起出去玩，很開心。

Content: Kipnis: I think you're, you're greatly overestimating my alcohol tolerance.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我覺得你，你大大高估我的酒量了。

Content: Schafer: All I heard is you are great.
Content_zh: Schafer：我只聽到你很棒。

Content: [Laughter] Schafer: It was like a team kickoff event.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Schafer：那就像團隊啟動活動。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Lipo was the very first code name of our very first project.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：Lipo 是我們第一個專案的第一個代號。

Content: This is the code name for Psychonauts.
Content_zh: 這是《Psychonauts》的代號。

Content: Rice: Never heard of it. What's that? Schafer: Let's go.
Content_zh: Rice：沒聽過。那是什麼？Schafer：走吧。

Content: Schafer: Buddha is the code name for Brutal Legend.
Content_zh: Schafer：Buddha 是《Brütal Legend》的代號。

Content: Hi, I'm Tim. Nice to meet you. What's your name?
Content_zh: 嗨，我是 Tim。很高興認識你。你叫什麼名字？

Content: Man: Peace again. Schafer: That's a weird name.
Content_zh: 男子：Peace again。Schafer：真奇怪的名字。

Content: Schafer: EZ5 is the code name for Happy Action Theater.
Content_zh: Schafer：EZ5 是《Happy Action Theater》的代號。

Content: Grasslands was our code name for Psychonauts II.
Content_zh: Grasslands 是我們給《Psychonauts II》的代號。

Content: Who knows if that'll happen. If that happens, we call it Grasslands.
Content_zh: 誰知道它會不會成真。如果成真，我們就叫它 Grasslands。

Content: Bow Bow was the code name for Brutal Legend II, we started on that.
Content_zh: Bow Bow 是《Brütal Legend II》的代號，我們有開始做那個。

Content: [Singing] Schafer: There's some documents called Bow Bow.
Content_zh: [唱歌] Schafer：有一些文件叫 Bow Bow。

Content: Vesuvio was Once Upon a Monster.
Content_zh: Vesuvio 是《Once Upon a Monster》。

Content: Empress is short for Empress of China and Empress was the sequel to Once Upon a Monster or Twice Upon a Monster,
Content_zh: Empress 是 Empress of China 的簡稱，而 Empress 是《Once Upon a Monster》的續作，或叫《Twice Upon a Monster》，

Content: it probably would have been called but that got cancelled.
Content_zh: 大概本來會叫那個名字，但它被取消了。

Content: But you still go there. It's important to go there and remember, pour out a drink for the cancelled projects.
Content_zh: 但你還是要去那裡。去那裡紀念很重要，為被取消的專案灑一杯酒。

Content: [Clinking glasses] Schafer: And last night we found what might be the last bar in Chinatown
Content_zh: [碰杯聲] Schafer：昨晚我們找到了可能是唐人街最後一家

Content: that we haven't named anything after and that's Red's.
Content_zh: 還沒被我們拿來命名任何東西的酒吧，那就是 Red’s。

Content: Schafer: Hey, here's to the Red's team. Red's.
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，敬 Red’s 團隊。Red’s。

Content: That doesn't sound bad at all. Red's.
Content_zh: 聽起來一點也不差。Red’s。

Content: Schafer: Code name Red's.
Content_zh: Schafer：代號 Red’s。

Content: [Music]
Content_zh: [音樂]
